title: paramignya andamanica
intro: Paramignya andamanica, also known as the Andaman paramignya, is a plant species native to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in India. It is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Rutaceae family, which includes citrus and related species. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which have been attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Paramignya andamanica include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in traditional herbal preparations, including tea and oil infusions.
